def transform(grid):
    R,C=len(grid),len(grid[0])
    from collections import Counter
    bg=Counter(v for row in grid for v in row).most_common(1)[0][0]
    result=[list(r) for r in grid]
    cross_val=1
    # Find 1-cross shapes (+ shape of 1s)
    cross_centers=[]
    for r in range(1,R-1):
        for c in range(1,C-1):
            if (grid[r][c]==cross_val and grid[r-1][c]==cross_val and grid[r+1][c]==cross_val and
                grid[r][c-1]==cross_val and grid[r][c+1]==cross_val):
                cross_centers.append((r,c))
    # For each cross, find the attached sequence and extend it
    for cr,cc in cross_centers:
        # Find sequence direction: check all 4 sides for non-bg, non-1 content
        for dr,dc in [(-1,0),(1,0),(0,-1),(0,1)]:
            nr=cr+dr; nc=cc+dc
            if 0<=nr<R and 0<=nc<C and grid[nr][nc] not in (bg,cross_val):
                # Found sequence start
                seq=[]
                r2,c2=nr,nc
                while 0<=r2<R and 0<=c2<C and grid[r2][c2] not in (bg,cross_val):
                    seq.append(grid[r2][c2])
                    r2+=dr; c2+=dc
                if not seq: continue
                period=len(seq)
                # Extend in same direction
                i=0
                while 0<=r2<R and 0<=c2<C and result[r2][c2]==bg:
                    result[r2][c2]=seq[i%period]
                    r2+=dr; c2+=dc; i+=1
    return result

def transform(grid):
    R,C=len(grid),len(grid[0])
    from collections import Counter
    bg=Counter(v for row in grid for v in row).most_common(1)[0][0]
    result=[list(r) for r in grid]
    cross_val=1
    # Find all cross centers
    cross_centers=[]
    for r in range(1,R-1):
        for c in range(1,C-1):
            adj=sum(1 for dr,dc in [(-1,0),(1,0),(0,-1),(0,1)] if 0<=r+dr<R and 0<=c+dc<C and grid[r+dr][c+dc]==cross_val)
            if grid[r][c]==cross_val and adj>=3:
                cross_centers.append((r,c))
    for cr,cc in cross_centers:
        for dr,dc in [(-1,0),(1,0),(0,-1),(0,1)]:
            # Look for sequence arm
            r2,c2=cr+dr+dr,cc+dc+dc
            seq_cells=[]
            while 0<=r2<R and 0<=c2<C and grid[r2][c2] not in (bg,cross_val):
                seq_cells.append((r2,c2,grid[r2][c2]))
                r2+=dr; c2+=dc
            if not seq_cells: continue
            seq=[v for _,_,v in seq_cells]
            period=len(seq); i=period
            # Continue from where seq ends
            while 0<=r2<R and 0<=c2<C and result[r2][c2]==bg:
                result[r2][c2]=seq[i%period]; r2+=dr; c2+=dc; i+=1
            # Also extend backward (toward cross)
            r3=cr+dr; c3=cc+dc  # adjacent to cross in same direction
            if 0<=r3<R and 0<=c3<C and grid[r3][c3]==bg:
                # extend sequence backward
                pass
    return result

def transform(grid):
    R,C=len(grid),len(grid[0])
    from collections import Counter
    bg=Counter(v for row in grid for v in row).most_common(1)[0][0]
    result=[list(r) for r in grid]
    cross_val=1
    # Find sequence cells (non-bg, non-1 cells that form lines)
    seq_rows={}
    seq_cols={}
    for r in range(R):
        for c in range(C):
            v=grid[r][c]
            if v not in (bg,cross_val):
                seq_rows.setdefault(r,[]).append((c,v))
                seq_cols.setdefault(c,[]).append((r,v))
    # For each row with a sequence, extend it in the same direction
    for r,cells in seq_rows.items():
        cells.sort()
        if len(cells)<2: continue
        # Determine period
        vals=[v for c,v in cells]
        c_min=cells[0][0]; c_max=cells[-1][0]
        period=len(vals)
        # Extend right
        i=0
        for c in range(c_max+1,C):
            if result[r][c]==bg: result[r][c]=vals[i%period]
            i+=1
        # Extend left
        i=period-1
        for c in range(c_min-1,-1,-1):
            if result[r][c]==bg: result[r][c]=vals[i%period]
            i=(i-1)%period
    for col,cells in seq_cols.items():
        cells.sort()
        if len(cells)<2: continue
        vals=[v for r,v in cells]
        r_min=cells[0][0]; r_max=cells[-1][0]
        period=len(vals)
        i=0
        for r in range(r_max+1,R):
            if result[r][col]==bg: result[r][col]=vals[i%period]
            i+=1
        i=period-1
        for r in range(r_min-1,-1,-1):
            if result[r][col]==bg: result[r][col]=vals[i%period]
            i=(i-1)%period
    return result

def transform(grid):
    R,C=len(grid),len(grid[0])
    from collections import Counter
    bg=Counter(v for row in grid for v in row).most_common(1)[0][0]
    result=[list(r) for r in grid]
    cross_val=1
    # For each sequence line (run of non-bg non-1 values in same row/col), extend
    for r in range(R):
        row=grid[r]
        runs=[]
        c=0
        while c<C:
            v=row[c]
            if v not in (bg,cross_val):
                start=c; vals=[]
                while c<C and row[c] not in (bg,cross_val): vals.append(row[c]); c+=1
                runs.append((start,c-1,vals))
            else: c+=1
        for s,e,vals in runs:
            period=len(vals)
            # Extend right
            i=0
            for c in range(e+1,C):
                if result[r][c]==bg: result[r][c]=vals[i%period]
                else: break
                i+=1
            # Extend left
            for c in range(s-1,-1,-1):
                if result[r][c]==bg: result[r][c]=vals[(period-(s-c))%period]
                else: break
    for c in range(C):
        col=[grid[r][c] for r in range(R)]
        runs=[]
        r=0
        while r<R:
            v=col[r]
            if v not in (bg,cross_val):
                start=r; vals=[]
                while r<R and col[r] not in (bg,cross_val): vals.append(col[r]); r+=1
                runs.append((start,r-1,vals))
            else: r+=1
        for s,e,vals in runs:
            period=len(vals)
            i=0
            for r in range(e+1,R):
                if result[r][c]==bg: result[r][c]=vals[i%period]
                else: break
                i+=1
            for r in range(s-1,-1,-1):
                if result[r][c]==bg: result[r][c]=vals[(period-(s-r))%period]
                else: break
    return result

def transform(grid):
    R,C=len(grid),len(grid[0])
    from collections import Counter
    bg=Counter(v for row in grid for v in row).most_common(1)[0][0]
    result=[list(r) for r in grid]
    cross_val=1
    # Find 1-cross shapes
    is_cross_center=[[False]*C for _ in range(R)]
    for r in range(1,R-1):
        for c in range(1,C-1):
            if all(grid[r+dr][c+dc]==cross_val for dr,dc in [(0,0),(-1,0),(1,0),(0,-1),(0,1)]):
                is_cross_center[r][c]=True
    # Find cells adjacent to cross that are sequence values
    for r in range(R):
        for c in range(C):
            if is_cross_center[r][c]:
                # Check each arm for sequence
                for dr,dc in [(-1,0),(1,0),(0,-1),(0,1)]:
                    # Skip the immediate arm (which is part of cross)
                    r2,c2=r+dr*2,c+dc*2
                    if not (0<=r2<R and 0<=c2<C): continue
                    if grid[r2][c2] in (bg,cross_val): continue
                    # Found sequence start
                    seq=[]
                    rr,cc2=r2,c2
                    while 0<=rr<R and 0<=cc2<C and grid[rr][cc2] not in (bg,cross_val):
                        seq.append(grid[rr][cc2]); rr+=dr; cc2+=dc
                    if not seq: continue
                    period=len(seq)
                    # Extend
                    i=0
                    while 0<=rr<R and 0<=cc2<C and result[rr][cc2]==bg:
                        result[rr][cc2]=seq[i%period]; rr+=dr; cc2+=dc; i+=1
    return result

def transform(grid):
    R,C=len(grid),len(grid[0])
    from collections import Counter
    bg=Counter(v for row in grid for v in row).most_common(1)[0][0]
    result=[list(r) for r in grid]
    # Each non-bg, non-1 connected component: find its direction and extend
    cross_val=1
    visited=[[False]*C for _ in range(R)]
    for sr in range(R):
        for sc in range(C):
            v=grid[sr][sc]
            if v not in (bg,cross_val) and not visited[sr][sc]:
                cells=[(sr,sc)]; visited[sr][sc]=True
                # BFS horizontally or vertically
                # Determine direction
                rs=set(); cs=set()
                rr,cc=sr,sc
                while cc+1<C and grid[rr][cc+1] not in (bg,cross_val): cc+=1; cells.append((rr,cc)); visited[rr][cc]=True
                for r2,c2 in cells: rs.add(r2); cs.add(c2)
                if len(rs)==1:
                    # Horizontal sequence
                    row=sr; vals=[grid[sr][c] for r2,c in sorted(cells,key=lambda x:x[1])]
                    c_max=max(c for r2,c in cells)
                    period=len(vals); i=0
                    for c in range(c_max+1,C):
                        if result[row][c]==bg: result[row][c]=vals[i%period]; i+=1
                        else: break
                else:
                    # Vertical (reset)
                    col=sc; vals=[grid[r][sc] for r,c2 in sorted(cells,key=lambda x:x[0])]
                    r_max=max(r for r,c2 in cells)
                    period=len(vals); i=0
                    for r in range(r_max+1,R):
                        if result[r][col]==bg: result[r][col]=vals[i%period]; i+=1
                        else: break
    return result

def transform(grid):
    R,C=len(grid),len(grid[0])
    from collections import Counter
    bg=Counter(v for row in grid for v in row).most_common(1)[0][0]
    result=[list(r) for r in grid]
    cross_val=1
    # Find the "extra" values on each cross arm (above/below the cross in col direction)
    for r in range(R):
        for c in range(C):
            if grid[r][c] not in (bg,cross_val):
                # Determine direction by looking at neighbors
                above=grid[r-1][c] if r>0 else bg
                below=grid[r+1][c] if r+1<R else bg
                left=grid[r][c-1] if c>0 else bg
                right=grid[r][c+1] if c+1<C else bg
                if above==cross_val or below==cross_val:
                    # Vertical sequence, extend upward if above bg
                    col=c; vals=[grid[r][c]]
                    r2=r-1
                    while r2>=0 and grid[r2][col] not in (bg,cross_val): vals.insert(0,grid[r2][col]); r2-=1
                    period=len(vals); i=len(vals)
                    rr=r+1
                    while rr<R and result[rr][col]==bg: result[rr][col]=vals[i%period]; rr+=1; i+=1
                    i=period-1
                    rr=r2
                    while rr>=0 and result[rr][col]==bg: result[rr][col]=vals[i%period]; rr-=1; i=(i-1)%period
    return result

def transform(grid):
    return [list(r) for r in grid]
